content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob play a game over NNN rounds, starting with an initial score of 000. Alice takes the first turn.

In the ithi_{th}ithâ€‹ round (1â‰¤iâ‰¤N1 \leq i \leq N1â‰¤iâ‰¤N), the current player must either add or subtract 2iâˆ’12^{i-1}2iâˆ’1 from the current score.

As the game progresses, let:


XXX be the maximum score reached at any point.
YYY be the minimum score reached at any point.
Aliceâ€™s goal is to minimize the final difference Xâˆ’YX-YXâˆ’Y, while Bob aims to maximize it.

Assuming both players play optimally, determine the final value of Xâˆ’YX-YXâˆ’Y after all NNN rounds. Since this value can be very large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists a single line of input.

The only line of each test case contains an integer NNN â€” the number of rounds played.




Output Format
For each test case,  output a single line containing one integer â€” the final value of Xâˆ’YX-YXâˆ’Y modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: Alice increases/decreases the score by 1, so the final value of Xâˆ’Y=1X - Y = 1Xâˆ’Y=1.
Test Case 2: Alice increases/decreases the score by 1. In his turn, Bob increases the score to 3 if the score after the first round is 1 or decreases it to -3, if it is -1. In either case, the final value of Xâˆ’YX - YXâˆ’Y is 3.
